title: Supply ranges of stone blocks used in masonry bridges and their construction period along the East Royal Road in the Khmer Empire, Cambodia
highlights:
masonry bridges and their construction  ...    During the Angkor period, there were fve Royal Roads linking the capital city of Angkor with provincial principal cities. Seven Temples d’étape, six Fire Shrines, and 25 masonry bridges were constructed along the East Royal Road to Preah Khan of Kompong Svay. We conducted measurements of magnetic susceptibility and chemical composition of laterite blocks and magnetic susceptibility of sandstone blocks used for the construction of the bridges to  ...    Keywords: Khmer monuments, East Royal Road, Bridge, Laterite, Sandstone, Chemical composition, Magnetic  ...    in and around Siem Reap, Cambodia, are now called the 
Angkor monuments. Te Angkor area was the center of the Khmer Empire and covers approximately 400  km2
. 
Conservation and restoration of the Angkor monuments have been conducted since 1907, especially by the École 
Française d’Extrême-Orient (EFEO). Te buildings of the Angkor monuments were constructed mainly with sandstone
